qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated full management API access and database exfiltration in default deployment
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding tests default disabled authentication (JWTAuthEnabled=false, BasicAuthEnabled=false, etc.), not the path traversal authentication bypass described in CVE-2026-0650. The advisory describes a specific vulnerability in the whitelist logic where strings.HasPrefix() without path normalization allows crafted requests like '/api/v1/health/../export/sqlite' to bypass authentication when it IS enabled. The finding's PoC does not enable authentication and does not test path traversal bypass. These are two distinct vulnerabilities: (1) default disabled auth vs (2) path traversal bypass when auth is enabled.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. CVE-2026-0650 describes an authentication bypass via path traversal in the HTTP middleware whitelist logic. The vulnerable code uses strings.HasPrefix() without path normalization, allowing crafted requests like '/api/v1/health/../export/sqlite' to bypass authentication when authentication IS enabled. The 'unauthenticated-management-api-access' finding tests a different vulnerability: default disabled authentication configuration, not path traversal bypass. This finding demonstrates access without credentials due to disabled auth, not bypassing enabled auth via path traversal.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-0650: Authentication bypass via path traversal in pkg/config/middleware.go whitelist logic
Advisory patch (commit fe83dc87aa40): Replaces strings.HasPrefix() with util.HasSafePrefix() that normalizes paths and blocks '..' traversal
Finding 'unauthenticated-management-api-access': Tests default disabled auth (JWTAuthEnabled=false, BasicAuthEnabled=false), does not enable auth or test path traversal
